executives.name: Ashley Martin
executives.title: Director, North America Ecommerce
executives.source_label: The Org — Brooks Running Org Chart
executives.relevance: PRIMARY TARGET. She directly owns brooksrunning.com conversion. Her own hiring posts use the words searchandising, wayfinding, personalization, and product discovery — exactly Algolia's value prop. She will feel NLP failures and empty states in her daily KPIs. She builds the business case up to Ryan Ngo.

executives.name: Ryan Ngo
executives.title: VP, North America Marketing & Ecommerce
executives.tenure: VP since ~2020 · at Brooks since 2013 (12+ years)
executives.relevance: ECONOMIC BUYER. Budget holder for a search platform decision. 12 years at Brooks — he built the ecommerce function from scratch and knows SFCC limitations intimately. Hashtags #ecommerce #sfcc in his own hiring posts. Ashley Martin is his direct report.

executives.name: Melanie Allen
executives.title: SVP, Chief Marketing Officer
executives.quote: The CDP is at the center of our tech stack...You've got all your data points that come into the CDP, which informs our marketing strategy, product roadmap, and customer service platforms.
executives.relevance: EXECUTIVE SPONSOR with a warm door. She already invested in Amperity CDP and measures click-through rate and conversion lift. Algolia + Amperity = search results personalized by CDP segments in real-time. This is a specific, differentiated angle: she has the data layer, Algolia is the search layer on top.

executives.name: Julia Payne
executives.title: Senior Manager, Digital Program & Delivery Management
executives.relevance: TECHNICAL ENTRY POINT. She is actively posting the Senior Engineer Digital Products role — she sits in the delivery org that would implement Algolia. Engage here for the SFCC integration and implementation timeline conversation once commercial interest exists at the VP/CMO level.

intelligence_signals.text: Brooks mission: 'At the center of everything we do is the runner.' But search results for 'best shoes for marathon training' redirect to generic bestsellers — not runner-first.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Brand promise gap — runner-centric philosophy vs. 2/10 NLP search score creates executive credibility risk.

intelligence_signals.text: Brooks reported 19% global revenue growth Q2 2025 driven by international expansion across 50+ countries.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Scaling to 50+ countries requires multi-language synonyms, geo-specific merchandising, and consistent sub-50ms latency worldwide — all gaps in current SFCC native search.

intelligence_signals.text: Brooks is actively hiring a Senior Engineer – Digital Products ($126,670–$202,714/yr) to 'evaluate vendors, RFPs, and integrate AI solutions' into Salesforce Commerce Cloud.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Technical Buyer role is currently VACANT. Engage IT leadership NOW before new hire establishes vendor preference. Ideal timing window for Algolia SFCC cartridge demo.

intelligence_signals.text: Claire Wood hired as VP of Footwear Product Strategy in February 2026 — new leadership typically triggers technology stack reviews within 90 days.
intelligence_signals.relevance: New executive = open window to question status quo. Frame Algolia as the upgrade aligned with her product strategy elevation.

intelligence_signals.text: Nike and Asics — two of Brooks' six largest direct running shoe competitors — both confirmed using Algolia (verified via BuiltWith, March 2026). Category leaders have chosen Algolia.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Golden angle: Brooks is the outlier. Asics and Nike use Algolia. Shoe Carnival (footwear) achieved +4.5% conversion with Algolia in first Cyber Week.

intelligence_signals.text: Brooks' current Salesforce Commerce Cloud native search has zero NLP capability. As customer expectations rise (driven by Amazon, Google Shopping), a 4.4/10 search score creates measurable conversion risk on $97M search-addressable revenue.
intelligence_signals.relevance: At 5% conversion improvement, Algolia addresses $4.85M/year. At 10%, $9.7M/year. Cost of inaction is measurable and growing with 19% traffic expansion.

intelligence_signals.text: Brooks already chose Salesforce Commerce Cloud (best-of-breed enterprise e-commerce SaaS) over building in-house. Algolia has a pre-built SFCC cartridge — 2-3 week integration path.
intelligence_signals.relevance: SFCC + Algolia is a proven integration pattern. Brooks' existing buy-oriented culture (Salesforce, agencies for paid media) removes the 'we want to build it' objection.

intelligence_signals.text: Two-layer verification: BuiltWith static scan flagged Constructor.io. Phase 2 network traffic inspection confirmed actual search calls route to Salesforce Commerce Cloud Demandware endpoint — NOT Constructor.io. Search provider is SFCC native.
intelligence_signals.source_name: Phase 2 Browser Audit — Network Inspection (SearchServices-GetSuggestions endpoint)
intelligence_signals.relevance: Constructor.io script tag present (possibly used for recommendations widgets) but is NOT the search engine. True displacement target is SFCC native search, not Constructor.io — this changes the competitive displacement pitch from 'replace Constructor' to 'upgrade from platform-native to AI-powered'.

intelligence_signals.text: Brooks committed to net-zero by 2040 and launched Brooks ReStart circular resale program. Search cannot currently surface ReStart products alongside new inventory — a federated search gap.
intelligence_signals.source_name: Brooks Running — People and Planet Path
intelligence_signals.relevance: Federated search across new + ReStart + sustainability content is a strategic differentiator unique to Brooks — not a generic pitch.

intelligence_signals.text: 38 open roles including Paid Media Specialist and China Digital & Social Program Manager — evidence of digital channel expansion requiring higher on-site conversion to justify media spend.
intelligence_signals.source_name: Lever Job Board — Brooks Running (38 roles)
intelligence_signals.relevance: Paid media ROI is capped by on-site search quality. Algolia's conversion impact directly justifies paid media investment — a champion angle for the marketing team.

intelligence_signals.text: Brooks Running created a new VP of Footwear Product Strategy role, hired in February 2026 — signaling product strategy elevation and likely tech stack review.
intelligence_signals.relevance: New executive VP in product strategy = 90-day technology review window. Frame Algolia as enabling her mandate: 'Give your product strategy a discovery engine that matches your R&D quality.'

intelligence_signals.text: Brooks Running delivered 16% full-year revenue growth in 2025, driven by international expansion across 50+ countries. Q2 2025 peak was 19%.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Confirmed full-year 16% revenue growth. Scaling internationally without scalable multi-language search is a compounding risk. Every new market adds search complexity.

intelligence_signals.text: Brooks Running is hiring a Footwear Merchandiser I (Lifestyle) in Seattle — a direct end-user of search merchandising tools who would benefit from Algolia Rules.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Merchandising champion signal. This person manages the Lifestyle footwear catalog and will feel Algolia's value most immediately — search result control, banner injection, product boosting.

intelligence_signals.text: SimilarWeb confirms brooksrunning.com receives ~5.25M visits/month (Feb 2026), 51.6% female audience, #8,951 global rank. Direct traffic = 39.5% — strong brand loyalty signal.
intelligence_signals.relevance: 5.25M monthly visits with 18% organic search share means ~945K sessions potentially affected by poor search relevance monthly. Paid search at 18.1% of traffic = search quality directly impacts paid media ROI.

competitors.name: Nike Running
competitors.notes: Category market leader. Uses Algolia for site search. AI-powered product discovery, AR foot measurement, multi-platform integration.

competitors.name: Asics
competitors.notes: Performance running leader. Uses Algolia. Contentsquare + Criteo analytics stack. Strong experimentation culture.

competitors.name: On Running
competitors.notes: Swiss premium brand, CloudTec cushioning. Shopify semantic search (vector embeddings). Fast growing D2C.

competitors.name: Hoka
competitors.notes: Deckers subsidiary. Hoka Shoe Finder quiz-based discovery (guided purchase). Maximal cushioning specialist.

competitors.name: New Balance
competitors.notes: Heritage brand. Strong technical filtering (width, pronation, drop). Comparison tool for serious runners.

findings.title: Zero NLP: Natural Language Queries Fail
findings.expected_behavior: Surface cushioned road running shoes (Glycerin, Ghost series) with distance/terrain filters
findings.actual_behavior: Redirected to /en_us/featured/best-sellers/ — generic bestsellers, zero intent parsing. Query not understood as product search.
findings.prospect_description: Brooks Running customers increasingly search using natural language — best shoes for knee pain, waterproof trail running shoes for women. Without NLP, these high-intent queries return zero or irrelevant results.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia NeuralSearch parses 'best shoes for marathon training' into filters: category=running shoes, cushioning=high, surface=road, distance=long. Returns Glycerin Max and Ghost 16 — not random bestsellers.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: 35% higher conversion rate with Algolia search (athletic footwear vertical)

findings.title: No Personalization: Every Runner Sees the Same Results
findings.tested_query: running shoes (after browsing Ghost 16 product page)
findings.expected_behavior: Ghost family boosted in results; Women/Men preference detected from browsing context
findings.actual_behavior: Results identical to fresh session — 54 products in default sort. No boosting of Ghost family. No gender preference detected.
findings.impact_stat: 70% of consumers are likely to complete a purchase if search results are personalized to their purchase history
findings.prospect_description: Brooks serves distinct segments — casual joggers, Boston Marathon qualifiers, physical therapists — each with radically different needs. Every visitor seeing identical ranked results means lost conversions across all segments.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia AI Personalization re-ranks results per individual runner: after Ghost 16 PDP view, Ghost family surfaces first. Pronation type and gender preference persist across sessions.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: +150% mobile order rate with Algolia AI personalization

findings.title: No Recommendations: PDP Shows Variants, Not Cross-Sell
findings.expected_behavior: 'Customers also bought' section with running socks, insoles, apparel
findings.actual_behavior: PDP shows 'Compare' section with Ghost 16 variants only (soft vs. firm cushioning). Zero cross-sell suggestions. No complementary products (apparel, accessories).
findings.impact_stat: 56% of online shoppers are more likely to return to a site that recommends products
findings.prospect_description: Brooks Running buyers frequently purchase complementary products (socks, insoles, apparel) — but without recommendations on PDPs, cross-sell and repeat-purchase revenue is left untouched.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Recommend shows 'Frequently Bought Together' (socks, insoles, run shorts), 'Similar Products' (Glycerin for cushion comparison), and 'Trending in Trail' for category cross-sell.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: +150% mobile order rate; Gymshark Recommend drives significant basket size increase

findings.title: Static Facets: Same Filters for All Queries
findings.expected_behavior: Women's-specific sizes and widths surfaced first; men's options de-prioritized
findings.actual_behavior: 108 results with same facet set as men's queries: Product Gender, Size, Product Type, Best For, Support Type — no dynamic adjustment for gender context.
findings.impact_stat: 41% of sites fail to fully support 8 key search query types performed by users
findings.prospect_description: Brooks carries shoes across multiple dimensions — surface, cushion, pronation, width, activity — but static filters force shoppers to scroll hundreds of results. Dynamic facets that adapt to query context would surface the right options instantly.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Dynamic Facets shows women's-specific widths (Narrow/Regular/Wide) when 'women's' is detected, hides men's-specific options, and surfaces size run appropriate to gender — reducing the cognitive load of 108 undifferentiated results.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: +50% search conversion with Algolia dynamic faceting and relevance

findings.title: Empty State Missing Trending Searches
findings.expected_behavior: Popular search terms (e.g., 'ghost 16', 'trail shoes', 'marathon training') to guide users
findings.actual_behavior: Empty state shows 'POPULAR PRODUCTS' product tiles (Women's/Men's shoes) but zero popular search queries, trending searches, or recent searches.
findings.impact_stat: 43% of site visitors immediately utilize the search box
findings.prospect_description: Nearly half of Brooks visitors go straight to search. Without predictive suggestions and smart no-results pages, a single misspelling becomes a dead end for the brand's highest-intent visitors.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Query Suggestions shows top 10 trending searches in real time (updated daily from analytics). When a runner opens search, they see 'ghost 16 is trending' or 'marathon training shoes' — guided discovery without typing.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: +50% search conversion; query suggestions reduce search abandonment

findings.title: No Promotional Banners in Search Results
findings.expected_behavior: Banner promoting Cascadia Elite launch or trail-specific promotion at top of results
findings.actual_behavior: 67 trail products returned (good breadth) but zero merchandising banners. 'Sale' query redirects to /en_us/sale/ category page instead of showing promotional content in results.
findings.prospect_description: Brooks has seasonal drops and running event promotions — but without merchandising rules in search, these campaigns are invisible to the 43% of visitors who go directly to the search box rather than the homepage banner.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Rules inject banners at the top of search results for specific queries: 'trail' shows the Cascadia Elite launch banner, 'sale' shows featured discounts inline, 'new' highlights the latest releases — without leaving the search results page.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: +37% conversion rate improvement with Algolia Rules and merchandising

findings.title: No Analytics-Driven Product Badges
findings.expected_behavior: Top search results show 'Bestseller' or 'Trending' badges based on search analytics
findings.actual_behavior: 79 results for 'ghost' — only 'Sale' (red badge) and 'Member Exclusive' visible. No 'Bestseller', 'Trending', or 'Most Searched' indicators.
findings.impact_stat: 93% of consumers have made a purchase after reading reviews
findings.prospect_description: Brooks Running has a loyal community who trust peer endorsements. Without bestseller badges or trending indicators surfaced in search results, the social proof that drives performance footwear decisions stays buried on individual PDPs.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Analytics powers real-time 'Bestseller' badges (by conversion rate), 'Trending' (rising search volume in past 7 days), and 'Most Searched' — all updated automatically from search data, no manual merchandising required.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: 35% higher conversion with Algolia analytics-driven merchandising

findings.title: Excellent Typo Tolerance
findings.actual_behavior: 20 Ghost 17 results returned — silent correction, no 'Did you mean?' friction. Typo handled perfectly.
findings.prospect_description: This is a strength — Brooks Running's typo tolerance correctly handles misspellings like "adreniline" and "ghost runing shoes", returning relevant results rather than zero results.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia's fuzzy matching would maintain this capability at sub-5ms latency while adding NLP, personalization, and recommendations on top of the same solid typo foundation.

findings.title: Federated SAYT: Queries + Products in Typeahead
findings.expected_behavior: SAYT dropdown with query suggestions and product results
findings.actual_behavior: SAYT shows 7 query suggestions + product tiles (Women's Revel 8, Men's Revel 8) + Popular Products section. True federated typeahead.
findings.impact_stat: Search autocomplete features are provided at 80% of e-commerce sites, yet only 19% achieve the highest performance
findings.prospect_description: Brooks Running's typeahead shows product suggestions only. A federated SAYT surfacing categories, editorial content, and shoe finder tools in one panel would serve the full range of runner intent.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia can enhance this federated SAYT with category shortcuts ('Women's Running Shoes →'), trending query indicators, and content cards (blog posts, fit guides) alongside products.

findings.title: Intelligent Informational Redirects
findings.expected_behavior: Content page surfaced (not a product search result page)
findings.actual_behavior: Immediately redirected to /en_us/returns/ — no product results shown, no search page. Clean intent detection for informational queries.
findings.impact_stat: 41% of sites fail to fully support the search query types that users perform
findings.prospect_description: Brooks has extensive editorial content — training plans, shoe guides, injury prevention articles. Informational queries like "plantar fasciitis shoes" or "how to train for a half marathon" hit a product-only dead end instead of finding the brand's expertise.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia can extend this to show help content alongside products in federated search — so 'return' shows both the policy page AND related return-eligible products in the same results view.

objection_counters.objection: Our current search is good enough
objection_counters.counter: Run competitive benchmark showing conversion rate delta vs category leaders. Nike and Asics — your direct competitors — both use Algolia. You're the outlier, not the standard.

SFCC search lacks AI, personalization, and federated capabilities — these aren't configuration options, they're architectural gaps. Lacoste and Puma both moved from SFCC native to Algolia for exactly this reason.
With $97M in search-addressable revenue, a 5% improvement pays back the investment in 15 days. What's the cost of NOT having best-in-class search while growing 19% internationally?
We integrate with Salesforce Commerce Cloud in 2–3 weeks. Your new Senior Engineer can own the RFP and integration while focusing on strategic projects — not months of custom build.

run_research_lab.algolia_angle: Brooks studies individual habitual motion paths — not generic averages. Search should do the same. Algolia AI Personalization surfaces results based on each runner's unique browsing and purchase signals, not generic bestsellers.

hiring.build_vs_buy_source_label: Senior Engineer — Digital Products JD: 'vendor RFP processes', 'third-party integrations', 'evaluating emerging technologies'

strategic_angles.label: Scale Your International Success
strategic_angles.hook: You achieved 19% growth through international expansion. As you enter new markets, can your search handle regional product preferences, language nuances, and local competitor context?
pain_points: Geo-specific merchandising (winter boots in Europe, trail shoes in Colorado)
International inventory visibility (don't show US-only products to EU customers)
strategic_angles.discovery_question: You announced 19% growth in Q2 driven by international markets. How are you ensuring runners in Germany get the same instant, relevant search experience as runners in Seattle? Are you able to merchandise differently by market today?

strategic_angles.label: Make Sustainability Discoverable
strategic_angles.hook: ReStart and your net-zero commitment are brand differentiators. But if customers can't easily find sustainable options, they can't buy them.
pain_points: Federated search across new + ReStart products in one experience
Custom ranking by sustainability attributes without hurting new product sales
Dynamic faceting for eco-friendly materials, carbon footprint filters
strategic_angles.algolia_proof: Algolia Federated Search — unified index across product types
strategic_angles.discovery_question: You've committed to net-zero by 2040 with Brooks ReStart. When a runner searches for 'sustainable running shoes' on your site today, do they see both new and ReStart options? Or are those siloed experiences?

strategic_angles.label: Research Lab-Quality Personalization
strategic_angles.hook: Your Run Research Lab studies individual motion paths — not generic averages. Your website should do the same.
pain_points: Personalization based on running style (neutral vs stability vs motion control)
Terrain preferences (road vs trail) not surfaced in search
Historical purchase patterns not informing recommendations
strategic_angles.discovery_question: Your Run Research Lab has 23 biomechanics experts studying individual motion paths. How do you surface those insights when runners search for shoes by pronation type or cushion preference today?

strategic_angles.label: Build-Quality Technology, Without Building It
strategic_angles.hook: You build shoes in-house because no one understands biomechanics like Brooks. But search isn't your core competency — partner with the experts like you do with Salesforce Commerce Cloud.
pain_points: Small digital products team (<5 engineers) cannot maintain search infrastructure at scale
Search relevance is never 'done' — requires continuous tuning
19% international growth requires search to scale without proportional engineering headcount
strategic_angles.algolia_proof: Salesforce Commerce Cloud cartridge — 2–3 week integration. Pre-built SFCC connector.
strategic_angles.discovery_question: You've partnered with Salesforce for commerce and agencies for media. What's your philosophy on build vs buy for search infrastructure? With a small Digital Products team, how much engineering time currently goes into search tuning vs product innovation?

icp_mapping.profile: Berkshire-backed private brand, 70% buy-oriented, SFCC-committed, small digital team, active international expansion

priority_to_product.their_priority: Net-zero by 2040 / Brooks ReStart circular program
priority_to_product.source: Brooks Running — People and Planet Path
priority_to_product.algolia_solution: Algolia Federated Search — unified search across new products + ReStart + blog content
priority_to_product.discovery_question: You've committed to net-zero by 2040 with Brooks ReStart. When a runner searches for 'sustainable running shoes' on your site today, do they see both new and ReStart options? Or are those siloed experiences?

priority_to_product.their_priority: Free Engineering for Innovation (implicit from hiring data)
priority_to_product.source: Senior Engineer — Digital Products job description
priority_to_product.algolia_solution: Algolia as managed infrastructure — no ML maintenance, continuous AI updates
priority_to_product.discovery_question: I see you're hiring a Senior Engineer for digital products to evaluate vendors and SaaS integrations. You already partner with Salesforce for commerce — what's your philosophy on build vs buy for search? Are you evaluating whether to build in-house or partner with a best-in-class search provider?

priority_to_product.their_priority: Tech Stack Modernization Window (Recent VP hire + Technical Buyer vacant)
priority_to_product.source: Brooks News page + Lever job board
priority_to_product.algolia_solution: Competitive displacement — Constructor.io is autocomplete-focused, Algolia is full AI search suite
priority_to_product.discovery_question: I noticed you recently hired Claire Wood as VP of Footwear Product Strategy in February. Often new leadership triggers a fresh look at the tech stack. With your Senior Engineer role also open, is this a good time to evaluate whether your current search provider is scaling with your 19% growth?

outreach_sequence.action: LinkedIn outreach to IT Leadership (hiring manager for Senior Engineer)
outreach_sequence.subject: Scaling Brooks' international search — how Nike and Asics do it
outreach_sequence.angle: 19% Q2 growth + search limitations at scale

outreach_sequence.action: Send Under Armour + Shoe Carnival case studies
outreach_sequence.subject: Athletic footwear peer: 35% conversion lift + 4.5% Cyber Week conversion increase
outreach_sequence.angle: Same vertical, same SFCC platform, proven ROI

outreach_sequence.subject: Quick audit of brooksrunning.com search — 3 gaps, 1 conversation
outreach_sequence.angle: Lead with F01 NLP failure ('marathon training') as conversation starter

outreach_sequence.action: Technical demo: multi-language search + SFCC integration path
outreach_sequence.subject: Live demo: Algolia + Salesforce Commerce Cloud, same stack you run today
outreach_sequence.angle: Geo-specific merchandising for international expansion + SFCC cartridge 2–3 week integration

anchor_lines: Brooks told runners 'at the center of everything we do is the runner' — Algolia makes that true in search
Your Run Research Lab studies individual motion paths. Algolia makes search do the same.
Nike and Asics — 2 of your 6 direct running shoe competitors — both use Algolia. You're the outlier.
You chose Salesforce Commerce Cloud over building your own e-commerce platform. Apply the same logic to search.
With $97M in search-addressable revenue and a 15-day payback, this isn't a nice-to-have — it's a Q3 decision.

competitive_synthesis.scenario_note: Brooks is behind category leaders on NLP/personalization (defensive gap) but ahead of or at parity with most running specialty peers (offensive opportunity). 2 of 6 direct competitors already use Algolia — making this also a golden angle play.

competitor_tiers.search_stack: Algolia (confirmed BuiltWith) + custom AI/ML acquisitions (Zodiac 2018, Select 2019, Datalogue 2021) + NikeAI Beta (iOS)
competitor_tiers.gap_vs_brooks: 2+ years ahead on personalization, NLP, AR-assisted discovery
competitor_tiers.strategic_implication: Brooks cannot match Nike's budget. Algolia closes the NLP and personalization gap. Brooks then wins on running-specialty depth — pronation filtering, biomechanics data, RunResearch Lab integration.
Asics chose Algolia for running specialty e-commerce — direct validation. Brooks faces Algolia in competition. Better to wield it than face it.

competitor_tiers.tier: DISRUPTOR — Neutralize Before They Pull Away
competitor_tiers.search_stack: Shopify native (semantic search with vector embeddings)
competitor_tiers.gap_vs_brooks: Modern Shopify semantic search; fast-growing D2C brand with strong digital investment
competitor_tiers.strategic_implication: On Running is the fastest-growing competitor in running specialty. Shopify's native semantic search gives them a baseline advantage over Brooks' SFCC native. Algolia leapfrogs Shopify semantic with NLP, personalization, and recommendations.

competitor_tiers.gap_vs_brooks: At or below Brooks' baseline. New Balance has strong technical filtering; Hoka uses quiz-based discovery; Saucony relies on SEO + basic faceting.
competitor_tiers.strategic_implication: Algolia gives Brooks a leapfrog capability vs these peers. NLP search, AI personalization, and real-time recommendations are capabilities none of these competitors currently deploy.

experience_debt.debt_description: Brooks is 4.1 points below Nike and 1.1 points below the parity cluster (New Balance, Hoka, Saucony). The 3 HIGH-severity gaps (NLP=2/10, Personalization=3/10, Recommendations=3/10) are precisely where category leaders have invested and where Algolia has the strongest case studies.

positioning_matrix.strategic_play: Defensive (close gap to Nike/Asics) + Offensive (leapfrog New Balance/Hoka)
Defensive (close gap to Nike/Asics) + Offensive (leapfrog On Running)
Leadership — sub-20ms is a category first in running specialty at international scale
Leadership — Brooks' Run Research Lab data + Algolia NLP = category-first capability no competitor can replicate

positioning_statements.defensive: Nike and Asics — your two most-tracked competitors — both use Algolia for NLP and personalization. Your current 4.4/10 search score means you're serving runners with 2018-era technology against 2026-era competition. Algolia closes that gap in weeks, not years.
positioning_statements.offensive: New Balance, Hoka, and Saucony are all running on platform-native search with no NLP, no AI personalization, no recommendations. Algolia makes Brooks the first running specialty brand with true AI search — a capability gap your parity competitors can't close quickly.
positioning_statements.golden_angle: You're already competing against Algolia — Nike has it, Asics has it, and On Running's Shopify semantic search is the closest native alternative. The only question is whether you deploy it or face it.
positioning_statements.leadership: Brooks knows runners better than anyone — 23 biomechanics researchers, 193 years of combined experience, individual motion path analysis. Algolia makes that knowledge instantly discoverable. No competitor can replicate the combination of Brooks' research depth and Algolia's AI. That's a search experience category leaders can't copy.

competitive_synthesis.the_ask: Don't just catch up to Nike. Leapfrog them. Algolia gives Brooks AI capabilities that even category leaders don't have yet — specifically, biomechanics-informed search that turns your Run Research Lab data into a discovery engine. Nike spends billions on AI. Brooks has 23 researchers who already understand runners better. Give those insights a search interface.

defensive.label: Revenue Protection
defensive.logic: Prevent customer loss to better search experiences. Runners who fail to find products on Brooks.com today may buy from Nike.com or Asics.com — both of which have AI search.

offensive.label: Revenue Growth
offensive.logic: Capture share from New Balance, Hoka, Saucony runners who experience friction on those sites. Category-first NLP and personalization attracts runners who've given up on lesser search experiences.

leadership.label: Market Expansion
leadership.logic: First running brand with biomechanics-informed AI search attracts new runners who value technology-backed fit recommendations. Supports Brooks' international expansion (19% Q2 growth) with multi-language, geo-specific search at scale.
leadership.est_annual: $14.55M (optimistic — 15% lift with full Algolia suite + Run Research Lab integration)

golden_angle.headline: 2 of 6 direct running shoe competitors use Algolia

competitors_using_algolia.name: Nike
competitors_using_algolia.verification: BuiltWith domain-lookup confirmed Algolia JavaScript on nike.com — March 17, 2026
competitors_using_algolia.significance: Category market leader. Nike chose Algolia for AI-powered product discovery.

competitors_using_algolia.name: Asics
competitors_using_algolia.verification: BuiltWith domain-lookup confirmed Algolia JavaScript on asics.com — March 17, 2026
competitors_using_algolia.significance: Performance running leader. Asics chose Algolia — proven in the running category.

footwear_case_study.result: +4.5% conversion rate increase during first Cyber Week post-Algolia implementation
footwear_case_study.context: One of America's largest footwear retailers (400+ locations). Implemented headless commerce with Algolia + Amplience.

golden_angle.positioning: Brooks is the outlier. The category leader (Nike) and category peer (Asics) have both chosen Algolia. Shoe Carnival proved 4.5% conversion lift in footwear. Brooks' current 4.4/10 search score puts them below where competitors started before they adopted Algolia.
golden_angle.talk_track: Nike and Asics — two of your six direct running shoe competitors — both confirmed using Algolia as of March 2026. A footwear retailer using Algolia saw 4.5% conversion lift in their first Cyber Week. You're not evaluating an unproven technology — you're evaluating whether to join the category leaders or remain the outlier.

methodology: Phase 1 research (March 17, 2026): BuiltWith 5-endpoint technology analysis, broker-level financial triangulation (private company — Berkshire Hathaway subsidiary), 38-role hiring intelligence via Chrome MCP browser automation on jobs.lever.co/brooksrunning. Note: SimilarWeb MCP returned 403 access error — traffic data unavailable. Phase 2 browser audit (March 17, 2026): 20 test scenarios executed in real Chrome (1440x900 desktop + 375x667 mobile). Network inspection confirmed SFCC Demandware search endpoint, not Constructor.io. 23 screenshots on disk. Scoring: 10 challenge areas, weighted formula (HIGH=2x, MEDIUM=1x, LOW=0.5x). Final score: 4.4/10.
